mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate compresses discount rates and encourages risk-taking in equities, lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC of FEDFUNDS level vs. forward 20-day returns is below 0.03, or the mean 20-day return in periods with FEDFUNDS at current low levels is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A very high unemployment rate signals severe labor market slack that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods when UNRATE exceeds 7.0%, the 5-day forward SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not statistically different from zero at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal a broad-based demand recovery that lifts corporate revenues and equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both RSAFS and HOUST show month-over-month increases
failure_condition: In months where both RSAFS and HOUST rise sequentially, the 60-day forward SPX return is not significantly positive (mean return ≤ 0, t-test p > 0.05), or the signal fails to produce a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production increases month-over-month during the post-crisis period signal that supply capacity is healing, which supports a positive S&P 500 forward return over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: During post-crisis phases when INDPRO rises, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the fed funds rate is near zero and nonfarm payrolls simultaneously rise, the combination of extreme monetary accommodation and improving employment produces strong positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with nascent recovery
failure_condition: In months meeting the trigger conditions, the 20-day forward SPX return is not positive in at least 60% of instances, or the mean return is negative (p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales rising above a key threshold indicate that consumer spending is strong enough to drive GDP growth, which pushes S&P 500 forward returns higher over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: When RSAFS exceeds 350000, the 20-day forward SPX return is negative more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not statistically significant from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production trending upward indicates expanding manufacturing activity that drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward SPX return following any increase in INDPRO is not positive on average (t-test p > 0.10), or the rolling 1-year correlation between INDPRO level and next-5-day return is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in housing starts signals a turning point in residential investment that leads to substantial S&P 500 gains over the ensuing 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following months with HOUST rising >5%, the 60-day forward SPX return is non-positive on average, or the signal yields a false positive rate over 50% in walk-forward backtests.
